items.fix: Add a `flake8-debugger` / `ruff` rule that fails on `breakpoint()` / `pdb.set_trace()`; pre-commit catches it.
items.why: An accidental breakpoint in production hangs the process when it's hit; a CI gate is cheap insurance.
items.example: Ruff: enable rule `T100`. Pre-commit hook fails the commit if found.

items.mistake: Using `s` (step) when you meant `n` (next), then getting lost in `int.__add__`.
items.fix: Default to `n`; only `s` when you actually want to enter the next function call.
items.why: Step-into descends into every callable, including standard-library internals — easy to lose your place.
items.example: If you're inside Python internals, `r` (return) brings you back to your frame.

items.mistake: Pausing inside code with a strict timeout (heartbeats, network).
items.fix: Stub out the timeout for the debug session, or use post-mortem after the failure.
items.why: While you're at the (Pdb) prompt, real wall-clock time still passes — heartbeats fail, sockets close.
items.example: Set very large timeouts in dev config, or write a fake client that doesn't enforce them.

items.mistake: Modifying state at the prompt and forgetting it doesn't persist after `q`.
items.fix: Use the prompt to *test* a hypothesis; then apply the fix in source code and rerun.
items.why: Quitting pdb doesn't save anything; the value of the session is in what you *learned*, not what you mutated.
items.example: Treat the (Pdb) prompt as a scratchpad, not a code editor.

items.mistake: Trying to debug async / multi-thread bugs with plain pdb.
items.fix: Use `aiomonitor`, an IDE debugger with thread support, or sprinkle logging instead.
items.why: Pausing one task / thread doesn't pause the others — the bug morphs while you're poking it.
items.example: VS Code's debugger pauses all tasks by default and shows the full task tree.
